back with the only living thing I love 😃
Are the answers serious or not?

Here, Google has decided to merge two of its frameworks: Angular and Wiz (yes, I'm hearing about Wiz for the first time). Here's what one of the Angular developers wrote about it on Hacker News:

> The tl;dr is that we see a lot of similar requirements from developers across Angular and Wiz, so we're looking for opportunities to reuse work. [...]

> Over time, we'll continue focusing on what's best for developers and incorporating the best from Wiz in Angular, and vice versa. At the end, we can end up with one framework, or continue to coexist. [...]

> We're building infrastructure for these products rather than the products themselves.

It seems reasonable, right? Did he forget anything? If he did, it was probably something not that important.

Of course, he forgot about the user. It's become a common trope: we try to please the developer, we want to impress the developer, but what the final application turns out to be is not that important. As long as we had fun.

Although it seems obvious that all of this is done for the user, and it should be evaluated primarily based on what kind of system results, rather than how convenient it was for the developer.

Who cares about developers

The difference between this collapse and previous collapses is that:

- This collapse will be global in its totality.

- We are falling from way higher so the damage will be higher as well (technological dependency, lack or farming knowledge, overpopulation, etc)

- The multirracial and multicultural societies will provoke lack of social cohesion seen in previous collapses. Actually, a racial war might take place.

- Lack of spirituality and religion.

- Generalized mental disorders.
We will make it together.
